title: IFFS Triennial Survey 2026
description:
The International Federation of Fertility Societies is pleased to present our website for collecting and managing information to be used in our triennial Surveillance publication.  

This survey will provide important information about your country's assisted reproductive technology policies, practices, and goals.

All information received through our forms and other communications is subject to our Privacy Policy.
completedHtml:
Thank you for completing your IFFS Triennial Survey form.


Dear {firstname-for-complete-page},
Your information has been successfully received, and we look forward to providing you with the highest level of support.

If you have any questions or need to schedule an appointment, please don't hesitate to reach out to our office. Our team is here to assist you every step of the way.

Warm regards,
IFFS Triennial Survey Team

pages.name: ART - Posthumous Reproduction
pages.title: ART - Posthumous Reproduction
pages.description:
For questions 104 and 105, an Immediate Posthumous Reproduction Procedure refers to the harvesting of gametes from a person who is in a brain dead state on a life support or has been declared dead within the previous 24 hours and is having gamete extraction within this time frame.

When the word “Immediate” is not used, post-humous reproduction refers to the use of cryopreserved gametes or embryos of a person after their death, where the original storage occurred when they were still alive.
